trade.summary: Trade between Indonesia and Messico is limited given geographic distance and lack of a bilateral FTA. Indonesia exports textiles, electronics components, and palm oil derivatives. Messico exports chemicals, machinery, and agricultural products. Both are G20 members and developing economies seeking to diversify trade.

diplomatic.summary: Indonesia and Messico maintain diplomatic relations and interact primarily at multilateral forums including G20 and the UN. Both are members of the MIKTA grouping (Messico, Indonesia, Corea del Sud, Turchia, Australia) which serves as a bridge between the G8 and G20. MIKTA engagement is the primary diplomatic framework.

military.summary: Nessuna relazione militare significativa. Scambi occasionali in forum di difesa multilaterali.

financial.summary: Very limited direct FDI flows in either direction. Geographic distance and lack of bilateral investment treaty constrain financial ties.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Both countries are net energy producers with limited interdependence.

scientific.summary: Modest academic exchanges facilitated through multilateral science programmes. Limited direct university partnerships.
